John Doe
Senior Software Engineer at Acme Corp
San Francisco Bay Area
500+ connections
About

I build distributed systems that handle millions of requests per day without falling over. Eight years of backend engineering — mostly in Go and Python — with a focus on observability, reliability, and keeping on-call rotations boring.

Currently at Acme Corp where I own the payments infrastructure serving 3M+ daily transactions. Previously at Nimbus and Pulsar Labs.

I care about clean APIs, thorough post-mortems, and code that junior engineers can actually read.

Experience
  • Senior Software Engineer
    Acme Corp
    Jan 2021 – Present
    San Francisco, CA
    • Redesigned payment processing pipeline to handle 3M+ daily transactions with 99.99% uptime
    • Reduced P99 API latency from 480ms to 62ms through query optimization and connection pooling
    • Led incident response and post-mortem culture improvements; MTTR dropped 55% over 12 months
    • Mentored 4 junior engineers through bi-weekly 1:1s and structured code review feedback
  • Software Engineer
    Nimbus Technologies
    Mar 2018 – Dec 2020
    Seattle, WA
    • Built event-driven microservices architecture in Go; handled 800K messages per day via Kafka
    • Implemented distributed tracing with Jaeger across 14 services; cut mean debug time by 40%
    • Contributed to on-call rotation for core data pipeline with zero missed SLA in 18 months
  • Junior Software Engineer
    Pulsar Labs
    Jun 2016 – Feb 2018
    Austin, TX
    • Developed REST API endpoints for a real-time analytics dashboard used by 500+ enterprise clients
    • Wrote automated integration tests that reduced QA cycle from 5 days to 1 day
    • Education
  • University of Texas at Austin
    B.S. Computer Science
    2012 – 2016
